source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two step-up transformers are connected in parallel to supply power to a load with an impedance per unit value of \( 0.8 + \mathrm{j} 0.6 \), as shown in the figure. Given \( \dot{U}_2 = 1 \angle 0^{\circ} \), transformer \( \mathrm{T}_1 \) is at the rated turns ratio, and transformer \( \mathrm{T}_2 \) has a high-side tap setting of \( +5\% \), with the impedance referred to the primary side as \( \mathrm{j} 0.1 \) per unit. Which of the following is the correct nodal admittance matrix \( Y_B \)?
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object placeholder, not a path/URL

multi_step: the task asks for a nodal admittance matrix, which typically requires deriving network parameters rather than direct lookup

question_text combines several conditions: parallel transformers, load impedance in per unit, one transformer at rated ratio, the other with +5% tap, and transformer impedance referred to the primary side
multiple-choice answers are close numerical matrices, suggesting intermediate electrical calculations and comparison are needed
verifiability: answer_text is a concrete 2x2 numeric matrix
choices provide explicit candidate matrices, enabling exact option matching
the quantities in the prompt are numerical and structured, so a downstream solver can recompute the admittance matrix
